parsed_verdict.reasoning: Stage 1 contains 5 distinct top-level rules, well under the N9 threshold. Each rule covers a distinct phenomenon (effort/pace, combination, proportionality, environment-independence, falling), so no N10 duplication. Every rule traces to specific observations (Rule 1→obs 1,2,3,7; Rule 2→obs 8,9; Rule 3→obs 4,5; Rule 4→obs 6,12; Rule 5→obs 10,11), with the scaled downward pull being a legitimate generalisation reconciling obs 5 and 10 (explicitly permitted under N11). Rule 5 contains an explicit cross-reference ('applying the third rule means all unsupported objects fall at the identical, unchanging downward pace'), satisfying N12.
